description:
You are a build-pipeline engineer who needs to generate a concise upload-status summary from yesterday’s Maven build log so that downstream automation can pick it up.

Existing data:
1. The raw build log is already present at  
   /home/user/builds/logs/build_2023-08-15.log
2. A reports directory already exists and is writable:  
   /home/user/builds/reports

What you must produce:
Create the file  
/home/user/builds/reports/2023-08-15_summary.txt  
containing exactly two newline-separated key–value pairs, in this order and with no extra whitespace:

SUCCESS_COUNT=<number_of_success_lines>
FAILURE_COUNT=<number_of_failure_lines>

Definition of log lines:
• A “success” line is any log line that includes the exact substring  
  “Uploaded:” (note the colon).  
• A “failure” line is any log line that includes the exact substring  
  “FAILED to upload:” (again, including the colon).

Formatting rules for the summary file:
• Use all capitals for the keys exactly as shown.  
• Both values must be integers written in base-10 with no leading zeros.  
• Each key–value pair must be terminated by a single newline character.  
• No additional text, spaces, or blank lines are allowed.

Goal:
Populate /home/user/builds/reports/2023-08-15_summary.txt with the correct counts derived from /home/user/builds/logs/build_2023-08-15.log. Accomplishing this correctly can be done with a single well-crafted shell command.

A quick verification tip (not required in your answer, but available to you): the final summary file should be exactly two lines long and should match the pattern  
SUCCESS_COUNT=[0-9]+  
FAILURE_COUNT=[0-9]+  

Remember: do not show the actual commands you use; just perform them in the terminal. When you are finished, the summary file must exist with the correct counts and precise formatting so automated tests can read it verbatim.
truth:
Pre-existing file:
/home/user/builds/logs/build_2023-08-15.log  
Contents (exactly):

[INFO] Uploaded: commons-io-2.11.0.jar
[INFO] Uploaded: guava-32.1.0.jar
[ERROR] FAILED to upload: junit-4.13.2.jar
[ERROR] FAILED to upload: log4j-1.2.17.jar
[INFO] Uploaded: slf4j-api-1.7.36.jar

Expected artifact the agent must generate:
/home/user/builds/reports/2023-08-15_summary.txt  
Expected contents (exactly):

SUCCESS_COUNT=3
FAILURE_COUNT=2

The automated test will read /home/user/builds/reports/2023-08-15_summary.txt and verify that it contains exactly the two lines above with no additional whitespace or newline characters after the second line.
name: task_000000_a1e6f1c8
